text: Discipline is the bridge between goals and accomplishment.
author: Jim Rohn

text: We are what we repeatedly do. Excellence, then, is not an act, but a habit.
author: Aristotle

text: The successful person has the habit of doing things failures don't like to do.
author: Thomas Edison

text: With self-discipline, almost anything is possible.
author: Theodore Roosevelt

text: Don't wish it were easier. Wish you were better.
Motivation gets you started. Discipline keeps you going.
Do it now. Sometimes later becomes never.
The pain of discipline is nothing like the pain of disappointment.
Small daily improvements lead to staggering long-term results.
Talent is cheap. Dedication is expensive.
You will never always be motivated. You have to learn to be disciplined.
One day or day one. You decide.
Stop managing your time. Start managing your energy.
The man who moves a mountain begins by carrying small stones.
author: Confucius

text: Endure. Then prevail.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: Discipline is not punishment. It is the highest form of self-respect.
Your habits will either make you or break you. Choose wisely.
The amateur waits for inspiration. The professional shows up and gets to work.
author: Steven Pressfield

text: No shortcuts. No excuses. No days off from who you are becoming.
An hour of pain in the gym is better than a lifetime of pain on the couch.
